name: MUCINOUS OVARIAN TUMOR

demographics.Frequency: 20% of all ovarian tumors; 2nd most common benign epithelial neoplasm of ovary
demographics.Histo: single layer of nonciliated tall columnar epithelium with clear cytoplasm of high mucin
demographics.Age: middle adult life, rare before puberty + after menopause

dominant_finding: high-attenuation proteinaceous material (20-30 HU) in some loculi
discriminating_features: multilocular cyst with numerous thin septa
cysts frequently have high protein content:

general: multilocular cyst with numerous thin septa
cysts frequently have high protein content:
multilocular with numerous smooth thin-walled cysts
solid tissue areas: thick septa + other soft-tissue elements within septated cyst
usually unilateral; bilateral in 5-10% of stage I cases
capsular infiltration with loss of definition + fixation
CT: high-attenuation proteinaceous material (20-30 HU) in some loculi
MR: variable signal intensity in different loculi ← proteinaceous / mucinous content,
